articles.title: In Depth: Repayment Question Looms as China Speeds Up Special Bond Issuance
articles.summary:
*   A 2024 audit found that 132.6 billion yuan ($19.5 billion) in local government special-purpose bonds (SPBs) were used improperly, with past audits also revealing inflated projections and idle funds.
*   Outstanding SPBs total ~40 trillion yuan, a key economic tool since 2015, raising repayment concerns if projects fail to generate sufficient returns.
*   A 2024 reform pilot shifted SPB approval to a local "self-review" system; pilot provinces achieved 93% quota utilization vs. 75% elsewhere, but now bear full repayment responsibility.
*   Since late 2024, SPBs are increasingly used for real estate (e.g., buying unsold homes), with Q1 2026 issuances up 42% year-on-year to 225 billion yuan.
*   Local government bond interest payments grew nearly 10% to 1.48 trillion yuan in the latest fiscal year, straining fiscal revenues.

articles.title: Shanghai Launches Citywide Hospital Escort Program for the Elderly
articles.summary:
• Shanghai will launch a citywide regulated medical escort service starting July 1, 2025, for all residents aged 60 and above, aiming to standardize a previously unregulated sector.

• The service addresses a critical national shortfall estimated at 830,000 to 2.2 million professionals, driven by China's aging population and a lack of occupational standards.

• Data shows the industry is young and under-qualified: nearly 60% of escorts have less than one year of experience, 90% lack systematic training, and only 17.21% have a medical background.

• Shanghai's regulations require escort agencies to register, maintain minimum staff levels, use formal contracts, and standardize pricing. Districts are developing platforms for booking and integrating services into elderly care subsidies.

articles.title: Fugitive Chinese Billionaire Guo Wengui Sentenced to 30 Years for Billion-Dollar Fraud
articles.summary:
*   Guo Wengui was sentenced to 30 years in prison and ordered to forfeit $889 million after being convicted in July 2024 on nine felony charges, including fraud and money laundering involving over $1 billion.
*   From 2018 to 2023, Guo used his GTV Media Group to defraud thousands of investors, promising guaranteed returns while spreading false information about COVID-19 and the 2020 U.S. election. He misappropriated the funds for personal luxury items, such as mansions and a yacht.
*   Judge Analisa Torres described Guo as the "top boss" of the scheme, rejecting his claims of a minor role. She criticized his exploitation of philanthropic purposes, intimidation of critics, and refusal to accept responsibility.
*   Prosecutors argued Guo is "a con artist, a scammer and a thief" who orchestrated the fraud to maintain his lifestyle after Chinese authorities seized his assets, concluding he will "always be a danger to society."
*   Guo, who intends to appeal, delivered a statement in court claiming he fell ill that morning. The sentencing had been postponed several times from its original November 2024 date.

commentary: The request was rejected because it was considered high risk
commentary_title: In Depth: Repayment Question Looms as China Speeds Up Special Bond Issuance
